author: @shortbuslife3440
text: I have some big concerns about the steering it looks like the way you've done that you will be leaning the handle bars rather than turning them.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well - I have to ride it so it's my problem really - if you build one do different steering lol - seriously though point taken and we will see - cheers mate

replies.author: @shortbuslife3440
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering just test it by pushing it while trying to make turns left and right first, I'm just raising my concerns having custom built racing motorcycle's and cars, but your call.

author: @RichardStansfield
text: Hi Rob, please be aware that working with galvanized material is hazardous. Welding in particular gives off carcinogenic fumes that go straight up into your welding helmet! So always use extractor. Cutting with the grinder is also iffy, again due to heating up the zinc plating. Sorry to put a downer on such an awesome project

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no worries mate - I always deal with this as advice for others and leave it there for that as I think it is awesome advice - myself - I just do what I do and am happy with it - so basically ignore it as I am old enough to feel I just don't need HSE advice - sorry mate but thanks too.

author: @dav1dbone
text: Amazing progress, it's like a commercial vehicle and would not look out of place in main dealer, just wondering if the rear wheel is mounted in reverse or is it intentional? Maybe you have already hooked it up to test direction, these controllers usually let you change rotation with the "self learn" wire anyway.

author: @annamolly1261
text: You may be interested to know that there's a blacksmithing trick where you can brass a steel surface. Basically you can heat up the area of the steel with a torch (~320-600C, black heat range) and with a pure brass brush wheel attachment buff the brass onto the surface.

author: @McRootbeer
text: Great to see how far it's gotten.  Hope you do a video on how you chose and sized your batteries for this application.  Have you considered using the laser cutter to engrave the FWG logo or a grip pattern onto the floorboard?
